search_terms_used: running injury incidence epidemiology systematic review pubmed
running related injury prevention RCT randomized controlled trial pubmed
running injury risk factors umbrella systematic review pubmed
injury prevention training load running systematic review pubmed
running injury prevention strength training meta-analysis pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: 4 meta-analyses (Lauersen 2014 exercise prevention, Lauersen 2018 dose-response, Videbæk 2015 incidence, Peterson 2022 biomechanical risk), 4 systematic reviews (van Gent 2007 epidemiology, Ceyssens 2019 biomechanical risk, Kakouris 2021 incidence/prevalence, Damsted 2018 training load), 1 umbrella review (Correia 2024), 1 RCT (Chan 2018 gait retraining). Running-specific in 8/10 papers. Covers epidemiology, risk factors, prevention interventions, and training load relationships.

papers.title: Incidence and determinants of lower extremity running injuries in long distance runners: a systematic review
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 17 published reports to determine incidence rates and identify determinants of lower extremity running injuries in long distance runners.

papers.title: The effectiveness of exercise interventions to prevent sports injuries: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 25 RCTs determining whether exercise interventions (strength training, stretching, proprioception, multicomponent) reduce acute and overuse sports injuries. Strength training reduced injuries by ~70%.

papers.title: Incidence of Running-Related Injuries Per 1000 h of running in Different Types of Runners: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyses quantifying injury incidence per 1000 hours of running across different runner populations (novice, recreational, ultra-marathon, track and field athletes) from 13 prospective studies.

papers.title: Gait Retraining for the Reduction of Injury Occurrence in Novice Distance Runners: 1-Year Follow-up of a Randomized Controlled Trial
papers.notes: 1-year follow-up RCT evaluating a 2-week real-time visual feedback gait retraining program on injury occurrence in 320 novice distance runners compared to treadmill control.

papers.title: Is There Evidence for an Association Between Changes in Training Load and Running-Related Injuries? A Systematic Review
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physical Therapy
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed evidence on associations between changes in training load parameters and risk of running-related injuries.

papers.title: Strength training as superior, dose-dependent and safe prevention of acute and overuse sports injuries: a systematic review, qualitative analysis and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Dose-response meta-analysis of 6 RCTs quantifying the relationship between strength training volume and acute/overuse injury prevention, establishing a dose-response curve for protective effects.

papers.title: Biomechanical Risk Factors Associated with Running-Related Injuries: A Systematic Review
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 16 prospective studies evaluating biomechanical variables as risk factors for running-related injuries in previously uninjured runners.

papers.title: A systematic review of running-related musculoskeletal injuries in runners
papers.journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed incidence, prevalence, anatomical distribution, and common pathologies of running-related musculoskeletal injuries across multiple study designs and runner populations.

papers.title: Biomechanical and Musculoskeletal Measurements as Risk Factors for Running-Related Injury in Non-elite Runners: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Prospective Studies
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 30 prospective studies evaluating over 100 biomechanical and musculoskeletal risk factors for running-related injury in non-elite runners.

papers.title: Risk factors for running-related injuries: An umbrella systematic review
papers.journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science
papers.notes: Umbrella systematic review synthesizing 13 prior systematic reviews covering 148 primary studies and 207 outcomes to consolidate and classify the full range of risk factors for running-related injuries.
